parents.notableLifeEvents: Josie was a gifted musician. During the silent movie era she played the organ at movie theaters to make money. She had 2 years of college at Capitol Business College in Bismarck, ND. Her mom had sent her there and she worked as a housekeeper for the owners of the school to help pay for it. She did not like it. She didn't like accounting and didn't like the business part. Josie's mom was 50 when she had her and was a bit embarrassed of Josie. When she was in 7th grade her parents sent her down by train by herself to Mount Marty and she was there in the summer because she was so active. She did go back to Glen Ulin to finish 8th grade from Sacred Heart school. After she graduated, she went to high school at Mount Marty for 3 years in Yankton SD. She did not like the experience. She told her kids she would never send them to boarding school. She worked in the kitchen there and learned how to make things like potatoes Florentine. She was an expert with the knife and could dice an onion with great skill. Josie was the youngest child of the family and the oldest was Mike Schaaf.

Pete was born at home, delivered by Mrs. Steve Dodenheffer. When Janice was born he worked in an aluminum factory in the state of Washington. He did many things including working on the Garrison Dam as a foreman of a finishing crew for cement forms. Tillie Dodenheffer was the oldest of the siblings, she was the oldest sister then it was Peter, Steven, Joe, August, Johnny was the youngest. Pete lost his birth certificate when the state Capitol of ND burned down.

grandparents.memorableStories: Gustins were the arrogant ones. Arno Gustin one of the founders of College of Mary. Ag reporter Al Gustin.

siblings.memories: She was mad because her confirmation name was Gertrude
June 29, born on Kent's parents wedding anniversary. Born in a car on the way to Richardton Hospital.

earlyMemories.firstMemory: Liked to be outside and her sister did not like to be outside. Milked cows when they were living in Dodge and liked to do it.
earlyMemories.favoriteToy: Her horse Grey and she hated men because uncle August was mean to her. Spot was the last dog she had as a kid. Johnny got him when he had rented a farm.

education.higherEducation: Elementary School started in Dodge, ND for first and second grade. She could read big books and the teacher did not know what to do with her. She was never in a group setting like her peers and thought maybe she was being punished. Janice was the only one sitting alone, and that is why she thought she was being punished. The teacher would have her go up to the front of the classroom and read to the class. Janice went to Sacred Hearts school in Glen Ulin for 3rd, 4th, 5th. They moved to Strasburg ND when she was in 6th grade as her dad had a road construction job there. In Strasburg for 6th grade the teacher was a nun and she was a nut according to Janice. The neighbors gave her a party when they moved there. Lawrence Welk was from there and his wife was a nurse and would be a nurse there at the hospital when he was on tour. The nun was a nut because mom never wrote notes and there was a girl who was very nice and had given her a note in class and mom did not know about the note. When the class ended for the day the nun had Janice stay after and accused her about the note and mom was a bad influence on this girl. The note asked Janice who was her boyfriend was, and mom did not have one. The nun did things like make Johnny Baumgartner, the class clown, wear a dunce hat and go down to kindergarten as that was where she thought he belonged. She started college at Bismarck Junior College, Speech and History and those credits transferred to UND.
